beforeAfter.heading: Before and after.
beforeAfter.subheading: Drag to compare. Each pair is one Videomancer program on the same source — no plugin chain, no laptop.

identitySelector.heading: Start where your patch starts.
identitySelector.subheading: Live camera, modular signals, HDMI, MIDI, audio — pick the workflow that fits what you already have.

hardware.heading: Real hardware.
hardware.subheading: Heavy enclosure, panel-mount jacks, solid knobs with end-stop feel. Built in the spirit of broadcast video electronics from the 70s and 80s.

bts.heading: Built by video freaks.
bts.subheading: Designed, assembled, and tested by hand in a small workshop in Portland, Oregon.

proof.heading: Why this instrument.
proof.ctaBody: Pick up the instrument. Power it on and start patching — the manual, the program library, and the Discord are there when you need them.

segments.title: Live Performance
segments.body: Turn any camera or HDMI feed into live motion graphics. Every control on the front panel is mapped — no menu diving.

segments.title: Glitch & Texture
segments.body: Hands-on glitch, color, and keying on any source. No laptop, no plugin chain.

segments.title: Format Converter
segments.body: HDMI, composite, S-Video, component, and LZX 1V modular — convert between any of them.

segments.title: Audio-Reactive
segments.body: Patch MIDI, CV, audio, or a USB controller into any of Videomancer's twelve Parameters. Your sound drives the picture.

proofCards.title: Plug in and perform
proofCards.body: Patch any HDMI, composite, or component source. Load a program. Start patching. No software, no plugin chains, no laptop.

proofCards.title: An ever-expanding library of effects
proofCards.body: Twenty-four programs today — broadcast color correction, keying, feedback, glitch, pattern generators — with more in every firmware release. Every update is free.

proofCards.title: Open source SDK
proofCards.body: Write your own programs with the open source Videomancer SDK. Share patches, trade ideas, and ask questions alongside artists and engineers on Discord and GitHub.

btsImages.alt: Videomancer boxes stacked in the workshop
